channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Protesters tee off against Trump and Musk in ‘Hands Off!’ rallies across the US
item.description: Thousands of demonstrators are rallying at sites across the U.S. against President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk. More than 1,200 “Hands Off!” demonstrations were planned in all 50 states Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu will meet again Monday on Gaza, tariffs and more
item.description: PALM BEACH GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump plans to meet with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday in what would be their second White House sit-down since Trump's return to office.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump urges Americans to 'hang tough' on tariffs plan as markets tumble
item.description: President Donald Trump has called on Americans to "hang tough" amid stock market turmoil over the last few days and as China retaliates with 34% tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NY public schools tell Trump administration they won't comply with DEI order
item.description: New York state school officials have told the Trump administration that they will not comply with its demands to end diversity, equity and inclusion practices, despite the administration’s threats to terminate federal education funding.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: JP Morgan predicts 2025 recession as Trump tariffs rattle market
item.description: President Trump's tariffs and their fallout will cause the U.S. economy to enter a recession this year, J.P. Morgan predicts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-lawmaker George Santos faces 7-year prison sentence for federal fraud, identity theft
item.description: Disgraced ex-congressman George Santos could receive 7 years in prison after pleading guilty to federal fraud charges. Prosecutors allege he stole from donors and misused campaign funds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump Labor secretary hits the road as tariffs shock the markets
item.description: Many economists say the Trump administration’s global tariff strategy could spur a recession at home.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Attorney General Ken Paxton’s former aides win $6.6 million in whistleblower case
item.description: Paxton vowed to appeal the “bogus ruling” that found he improperly fired the four plaintiffs after they reported him to the FBI on allegations of corruption.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels And Jasper Scherer, KPRC 2

item.title: China hit brakes on TikTok deal after Trump announced wide-ranging tariffs, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said he is signing an executive order to keep TikTok running in the U.S. for another 75 days to give his administration more time to broker a deal to bring the social media platform under American ownership.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, AAMER MADHANI and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from dismantling agency that funds community groups in Latin American countries
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge agreed on Friday to block the Trump administration from dismantling an independent agency that distributes grant money to community development groups in Latin American and Caribbean countries. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chinese-owned GNC stores operating on US military bases spark national security concerns
item.description: Rep. Pat Harrigan warns GNC’s Chinese ownership poses a national security threat as the company operates 85 stores on U.S. military bases without disclosing ownership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: About 500 law firms sign brief challenging Trump's executive orders targeting the legal community
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive orders targeting the legal community pose “a grave threat to our system of constitutional governance and to the rule of law itself,” according to a court filing submitted Friday by more than 500 law firms.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dem AGs sue Trump admin over order eliminating several federal agencies
item.description: Twenty-one Democratic state attorneys general sued President Trump Friday over his executive order that aims to eliminate seven federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's tariffs hit uninhabited islands filled with penguins
item.description: The implementation of tariffs on the islands did not go unnoticed by Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Senate GOP approves framework for Trump's tax breaks and spending cuts after late-night session
item.description: The Republican-controlled Senate has approved a budget framework that includes multitrillion-dollar tax breaks and spending cuts.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A Pentagon inspector general announces investigation into Hegseth’s use of Signal
item.description: The announcement comes after lawmakers voiced concern about whether top national security officials discussed classified information on Signal about a military strike against Houthi rebels in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats outnumber Republicans by just 2 voters amid statewide switch to nonpartisan
item.description: After Nevada Republicans surpassed their Democratic counterparts in voter registration for the first time in nearly 20 years, Democrats have once again taken over as the state's top party -- with a voter registration lead of two.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Auditor reports that Minnesota muni liquor store profits up, in part due to THC offerings
item.description: Minnesota State Auditor Julie Blaha issued her annual report on municipal-run liquor store sales. It was the first include sales of hemp-derived THC beverages and gummies, and operators suggested those items contributed to store profits in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Federal judge drops corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: Adams was scheduled to go on trial in April until new leadership at the Justice Department under the Trump administration ordered prosecutors in New York in February to drop the case, sparking a public outcry and resignations of prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas and Carrie Johnson, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams federal bribery case dismissed
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams' federal bribery case has been dismissed, court records show. In a letter filed Wednesday, Judge Dale Ho ordered the case dropped with prejudice, meaning the government cannot bring these charges against Adams again in the future.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: New York City mayoral candidates vow to address rising antisemitism
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The leading candidates for New York City mayor say they have plans to fight a rise in antisemitism. One in 10 New Yorkers is Jewish, making it the largest Jewish population outside of Israel. Former Gov.
item.dc:creator: Anthony DiLorenzo, PIX 11

item.title: Ohio school district reverts to birth names, catching students, teachers off guard
item.description: Students at Columbus City Schools with preferred names in the district's system had their names reverted back to match their birth certificates.
item.dc:creator: Delaney Ruth, Fox 5

item.title: Yale professor leaving US: Trump launching 'brutal attack' on freedom
item.description: A professor at Yale University said Monday he would be leaving his post and the country over the Trump administration’s “brutal attack” on free speech.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says he sent MAGA influencer Ashley St. Clair $2.5 million, open to paternity test
item.description: Musk’s comments came after St. Clair was seen selling her Tesla, saying she was doing so after Musk allegedly cut childcare payments.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Legislation aimed at protecting Florida state parks stuck in committee
item.description: What all started with a leaked proposal to build golf courses and pickleball courts at state parks last year has now resulted in legislation that aims to protect natural grounds from those very acts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Crime, housing and Cuomo in focus in race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates on the campaign trail for New York City mayor are zeroing in on crime and affordability, as a new accusation against former Gov. Andrew Cuomo bubbles to the surface.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘not joking’ about a third term. Republicans insist he is
item.description: Congressional Republicans are brushing off President Trump’s latest comments about seeking a third term, downplaying them as a joke designed to needle the media even as the president insists he is serious.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House to remove magnolia tree dating to Andrew Jackson
item.description: Trump added that White House staff will preserve the Magnolia tree’s “Historic wood,” which, Trump said, “may be used for other high and noble purposes!!!”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
